definitions: To worsen an already difficult or bad situation by doing something else that makes it even more unpleasant or upsetting.
To make a bad situation worse by doing something that offends or humiliates someone.
examples: First, my car broke down, and then to add insult to injury, I got a parking ticket.
He lost the game, and then his opponent refused to shake his hand, which just added insult to injury.
They cut our salaries, and then to add insult to injury, they expected us to work overtime for free.
